title: peptide_allosteric_cross_v1 — CROSS-AXIS BB3 output rows
description: Output schema for _python_bridge/module/peptide_allosteric_cross.py. CROSS-AXIS integration BB3: the PEPTIDE :> WEAVE sub-axis (Zimm-Bragg helix-coil cooperative pre-organization; Zimm & Bragg 1959; Lifson & Roig 1961) and the ALLOSTERIC :> QUANTUM sub-axis (Monod-Wyman-Changeux two-state ternary-complex equilibrium; Monod, Wyman & Changeux 1965) are unified: a helical BH3-mimetic peptide (PEPTIDE axis) acts as an MWC R-state stabilizer (ALLOSTERIC axis) of an apoptotic regulator such as a BCL-2 family protein. The peptide's fractional helicity theta_H GATES the modality — a defined helix is required for the BH3 cleft to recognize the recognition helix; a low-helicity disordered peptide cannot present its hydrophobic-i,i+3,i+4 face and the MWC R-state stabilization is weak. Per row: peptide name, theta_H, MWC cooperativity factor log10(alpha), modulation classification (effective / weak / ineffective). Both sub-axis libraries are IMPORTED, not re-implemented (AGENTS.tape f3 — no shadow of sister logic). RESEARCH-STAGE HONESTY: stapled BH3-mimetic peptides are research-stage / early-clinical (Walensky et al., Science 305:1466, 2004 SAHB; ALRN-6924 against MDM2 — clinical trials; no FDA-approved stapled-peptide allosteric drug as of writing). The peptide-allosteric modality has SOME clinical precursors (semaglutide is FDA-approved but not specifically allosteric — peptide-precedent only; somatostatin analogs e.g. octreotide; maraviroc / asciminib are FDA-approved allosteric small molecules — allosteric-precedent only), but the helical-peptide × allosteric COMBINATION here is a research-stage modeling exercise. In-silico simulator-consistency only (AGENTS.tape g8/f2). PEPTIDE remains a SUB-AXIS :> WEAVE; ALLOSTERIC remains a SUB-AXIS :> QUANTUM; a CROSS is NOT a new axis. No quantity is derived from the n=6 lattice (f_lattice_fit / lattice-is-tool).

peptide.description: Model peptide identifier from the deterministic BH3-mimetic-class panel.

sequence.description: Single-letter amino-acid sequence of the model helical BH3-mimetic peptide.

n_residues.description: Number of residues in the peptide (kept small for exact 2^N Zimm-Bragg partition enumeration).

modality_note.description: Illustrative modality note describing the helical-peptide-as-MWC-R-state-stabilizer class.

drug_precedent_peptide.description: Own peptide-modality precedent (g3/f1 — described by precedent, never lattice-derived). Examples: semaglutide (FDA-approved peptide, NOT specifically allosteric — peptide-precedent only); somatostatin analogs (octreotide etc.); Walensky lab stapled-peptide research.

drug_precedent_allosteric.description: Own allosteric-modality precedent (g3/f1). Examples: maraviroc (FDA-approved allosteric CCR5 antagonist); asciminib (FDA-approved allosteric BCR-ABL1 inhibitor — myristoyl pocket). Small-molecule allosteric precedents — NOT peptide-allosteric efficacy claim.

structural_anchor.description: Structural / mechanism literature anchor for the helical BH3-mimetic-as-allosteric-R-state-stabilizer modality (e.g. Walensky et al., Science 305:1466, 2004 SAHB on BAX/BAK).

rt_kcal_per_mol.description: Thermal energy RT (kcal/mol) at the reference temperature.

sigma_nucleation.description: Zimm-Bragg helix-nucleation parameter sigma (<< 1 makes the helix-coil transition cooperative). Inherited from peptide_sim.

partition_Z.description: Zimm-Bragg partition function Z = Sigma sigma^(#nucleations) * Prod s_i — must be strictly positive.

fractional_helicity_theta_h.description: Boltzmann-weighted Zimm-Bragg fractional helicity theta_H in [0,1]. The defining real limit of the PEPTIDE sub-axis — a fraction cannot leave [0,1].

independent_residue_helicity_sigma_to_1.description: sigma -> 1 (no nucleation cost) independent-residue baseline helicity (theta_H_indep = mean s_i/(1+s_i)), the closed-form cross-check (theta_H <= this baseline always).

cooperativity_suppresses_helix.description: True iff theta_H <= independent-residue baseline (a nucleation penalty can only suppress helix).

mwc_alpha_cooperativity.description: MWC cooperativity factor alpha mapped from the peptide's helicity theta_H. By construction alpha = 1 + (alpha_max - 1) * theta_H so alpha in (1, alpha_max] for theta_H in (0, 1]. alpha > 1 = PAM/R-state stabilizer; alpha -> 1 as theta_H -> 0 (no helix -> no MWC effect).

mwc_log10_alpha.description: log10 of mwc_alpha_cooperativity — used to classify modulation as PAM / NAM / neutral via the same NEUTRAL_LOG_ALPHA_TOL imported from allosteric_sim (f3).

mwc_modulation_kind.description: MWC modulation classification using allosteric_sim's NEUTRAL_LOG_ALPHA_TOL (imported, not re-implemented — f3). Helical BH3 peptides map to PAM (R-state stabilizers) when theta_H is high enough to produce log10(alpha) > tol; otherwise neutral.

modulator_kd_uM.description: Modulator dissociation constant at the allosteric site (uM) used by allosteric_sim.affinity_shift to project the saturable EC50 shift.

assay_concentration_uM.description: Probe concentration (uM) at which the MWC affinity shift is reported.

allosteric_ceiling_shift_1_over_alpha.description: Saturable allosteric ceiling 1/alpha — the real-limit bound on the affinity-shift ratio (Christopoulos & Kenakin, Pharmacol. Rev. 54:323, 2002). For PAM (alpha > 1) the ceiling is < 1; for NAM (alpha < 1) the ceiling is > 1.

ec50_shift_ratio_at_assay_conc.description: MWC affinity-shift ratio (1 + [B]/K_B) / (1 + alpha * [B]/K_B) projected from allosteric_sim.affinity_shift (imported, not re-implemented — f3). Bounded between 1 (no modulator) and 1/alpha (ceiling).

allosteric_ceiling_respected.description: True iff the projected MWC affinity shift respects the saturable ceiling 1/alpha — the hard real limit of allostery (allosteric_sim C2 criterion).

helix_gate_theta_h.description: Illustrative helix-content gate: a defined helix is required for BH3-cleft recognition. modulation_classification = 'effective' iff theta_H >= this gate AND mwc_modulation_kind == 'PAM'. Not lattice-derived; not fit to data.

modulation_classification.description: Combined helix-gate + MWC classification: 'effective' = theta_H >= helix_gate AND PAM; 'weak' = theta_H below gate but PAM (defined helix lacking); 'ineffective' = mwc_modulation_kind != 'PAM' (no MWC R-state stabilization).

real_limit_anchor.description: Combined real-limit anchor string: Zimm-Bragg helix-coil + MWC two-state. Two coincident statistical-mechanical real limits, each from its own sub-axis.

real_limit_citations.description: Citations: Zimm & Bragg 1959; Lifson & Roig 1961; Monod, Wyman & Changeux 1965; plus stapled-BH3 / SAHB structural anchor (Walensky et al. Science 305:1466 2004).

in_silico_caveat.description: Restatement that every row is in-silico simulator-consistency only (g8/f2) — not a binding-affinity, potency, immunogenic or therapeutic claim.

comparison_is_ranking.description: Always false — rows describe a per-peptide helix-content/MWC pair, NOT a ranking of one peptide over another.

creates_a_new_axis.description: Always false — this cross rests on existing PEPTIDE :> WEAVE + ALLOSTERIC :> QUANTUM sub-axes; the hexa-bio core-5 set (QUANTUM, WEAVE, NANOBOT, RIBOZYME, VIROCAPSID) is UNCHANGED.

illustrative_only.description: Always true — the peptide panel, the theta_H -> alpha mapping, the helix gate, K_B and assay concentration are illustrative modeling choices, not fits to a specific stapled BH3-mimetic peptide or a specific BCL-2-family target.

research_stage_modality.description: Always true — the helical-peptide x allosteric COMBINATION is a research-stage modality. Stapled BH3-mimetic peptides are research-stage / early-clinical at best (Walensky 2004 SAHB; ALRN-6924 vs MDM2 — clinical). No FDA-approved stapled-peptide allosteric drug exists. Peptide-precedent (semaglutide; somatostatin analogs) and allosteric-precedent (maraviroc; asciminib) are cited as own MODALITY precedents only — neither is a peptide-allosteric efficacy claim.
